CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-18-2015 Continued To Dec-02-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration
Off Calendar Apr-23-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS HUBB FILTERS, INC., CHRISTOPHER HUBBARD, JEFF MORGEN, AND GARY NELSON'S Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration and Stay Proceeding is Granted on the condition that neither JAMS nor the arbitrator require plaintiff to pay any expenses beyond what he would be required to pay had this motion not be granted. The majority of the claims asserted by Mr. Long are covered by the parties' arbitration agreement. Because the promissory note claim falls outside the parties' arbitration agreement, that claim is stayed pending the results of the arbitration. By referencing the JAMS employment arbitration rules and procedures in their agreement, which in turn unmistakably disclose that arbitrability and validity of the arbitration clause are to be decided by the arbitrator, the parties delegated the issue of enforceability of the arbitration agreement to the arbitrator. (Universal Protection Service, L.P. v. Superior Court (2015) 234 Cal. App. 4th 1128). The Armendariz requirement that "when an employer imposes mandatory arbitration ' the arbitration ' process cannot generally require the employee to bear any type of expense that the employee would not be required to bear if he or she were free to bring the action in court" is applicable to plaintiff's claims and remains good law. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Irene L. Tofft, CSR # 12913
